search_text: A Multi-Domain VNE Algorithm based on Load Balancing in the IoT networks 物联网网络中基于负载均衡的多域VNE算法 Virtual network embedding is one of the key problems of network virtualization. Since virtual network mapping is an NP-hard problem, a lot of research has focused on the evolutionary algorithm's masterpiece genetic algorithm. However, the parameter setting in the traditional method is too dependent on experience, and its low flexibility makes it unable to adapt to increasingly complex network environments. In addition, link-mapping strategies that do not consider load balancing can easily cause link blocking in high-traffic environments. In the IoT environment involving medical, disaster relief, life support and other equipment, network performance and stability are particularly important. Therefore, how to provide a more flexible virtual network mapping service in a heterogeneous network environment with large traffic is an urgent problem. Aiming at this problem, a virtual network mapping strategy based on hybrid genetic algorithm is proposed. This strategy uses a dynamically calculated cross-probability and pheromone-based mutation gene selection strategy to improve the flexibility of the algorithm. In addition, a weight update mechanism based on load balancing is introduced to reduce the probability of mapping failure while balancing the load. Simulation results show that the proposed method performs well in a number of performance metrics including mapping average quotation, link load balancing, mapping cost-benefit ratio, acceptance rate and running time. 虚拟网络嵌入是网络虚拟化的关键问题之一。由于虚拟网络映射是一个NP难问题，因此很多研究都集中在进化算法的代表作遗传算法上。但传统方法的参数设置过于依赖经验，灵活性低，无法适应日益复杂的网络环境。另外，不考虑负载均衡的链路映射策略在大流量环境下很容易造成链路阻塞。在涉及医疗、救灾、生命保障等设备的物联网环境中，网络性能和稳定性尤为重要。因此，如何在大流量的异构网络环境下提供更加灵活的虚拟网络映射服务是一个迫切需要解决的问题。针对该问题，提出一种基于混合遗传算法的虚拟网络映射策略。该策略采用动态计算交叉概率和基于信息素的突变基因选择策略来提高算法的灵活性。此外，引入基于负载均衡的权值更新机制，在均衡负载的同时降低映射失败的概率。仿真结果表明，该方法在映射平均报价、链路负载均衡、映射成本效益比、接受率和运行时间等多项性能指标上均表现良好。 本文提出一种基于负载均衡的多领域虚拟网络嵌入算法，解决物联网环境中虚拟网络映射的灵活性和稳定性问题。 Peiying Zhang,Fanglin Liu,Chunxiao Jiang,Abderrahim Benslimane,Juan-Luis Gorricho,Joan Serrat-Fernacute cs.NI,cs.AI

authors_text: Awais Aziz Shah,Ahsan Adeel,Jawad Ahmad,Ahmed Al-Dubai,Mandar Gogate,Abhijeet Bishnu,Muhammad Diyan,Tassadaq Hussain,Kia Dashtipour,Tharm Ratnarajah,Amir Hussain
title: A Novel Chaos-based Light-weight Image Encryption Scheme for Multi-modal Hearing Aids
title_zh: 一种新颖的基于混沌的轻量级多模态助听器图像加密方案
abstract: Multimodal hearing aids (HAs) aim to deliver more intelligible audio in noisy environments by contextually sensing and processing data in the form of not only audio but also visual information (e.g. lip reading). Machine learning techniques can play a pivotal role for the contextually processing of multimodal data. However, since the computational power of HA devices is low, therefore this data must be processed either on the edge or cloud which, in turn, poses privacy concerns for sensitive user data. Existing literature proposes several techniques for data encryption but their computational complexity is a major bottleneck to meet strict latency requirements for development of future multi-modal hearing aids. To overcome this problem, this paper proposes a novel real-time audio/visual data encryption scheme based on chaos-based encryption using the Tangent-Delay Ellipse Reflecting Cavity-Map System (TD-ERCS) map and Non-linear Chaotic (NCA) Algorithm. The results achieved against different security parameters, including Correlation Coefficient, Unified Averaged Changed Intensity (UACI), Key Sensitivity Analysis, Number of Changing Pixel Rate (NPCR), Mean-Square Error (MSE), Peak Signal to Noise Ratio (PSNR), Entropy test, and Chi-test, indicate that the newly proposed scheme is more lightweight due to its lower execution time as compared to existing schemes and more secure due to increased key-space against modern brute-force attacks.
summary: Multimodal hearing aids (HAs) aim to deliver more intelligible audio in noisy environments by contextually sensing and processing data in the form of not only audio but also visual information (e.g. lip reading). Machine learning techniques can play a pivotal role for the contextually processing of multimodal data. However, since the computational power of HA devices is low, therefore this data must be processed either on the edge or cloud which, in turn, poses privacy concerns for sensitive user data. Existing literature proposes several techniques for data encryption but their computational complexity is a major bottleneck to meet strict latency requirements for development of future multi-modal hearing aids. To overcome this problem, this paper proposes a novel real-time audio/visual data encryption scheme based on chaos-based encryption using the Tangent-Delay Ellipse Reflecting Cavity-Map System (TD-ERCS) map and Non-linear Chaotic (NCA) Algorithm. The results achieved against different security parameters, including Correlation Coefficient, Unified Averaged Changed Intensity (UACI), Key Sensitivity Analysis, Number of Changing Pixel Rate (NPCR), Mean-Square Error (MSE), Peak Signal to Noise Ratio (PSNR), Entropy test, and Chi-test, indicate that the newly proposed scheme is more lightweight due to its lower execution time as compared to existing schemes and more secure due to increased key-space against modern brute-force attacks.
abstract_zh: 多模态助听器 (HA) 旨在通过上下文感知和处理音频和视觉信息（例如唇读）形式的数据，在嘈杂的环境中提供更清晰的音频。机器学习技术可以在多模式数据的上下文处理中发挥关键作用。然而，由于HA设备的计算能力较低，因此这些数据必须在边缘或云端处理，这反过来又给敏感用户数据带来了隐私问题。现有文献提出了多种数据加密技术，但它们的计算复杂性是满足未来多模式助听器开发的严格延迟要求的主要瓶颈。为了克服这个问题，本文提出了一种基于混沌加密的新型实时音频/视频数据加密方案，该方案使用切线延迟椭圆反射腔图系统（TD-ERCS）图和非线性混沌（NCA）算法。针对不同安全参数（包括相关系数、统一平均变化强度（UACI）、密钥灵敏度分析、像素变化数（NPCR）、均方误差（MSE）、峰值信噪比（PSNR）、熵测试和Chi测试）获得的结果表明，新提出的方案由于其执行时间比现有方案更短而更加轻量级，并且由于增加了针对现代暴力攻击的密钥空间而更加安全。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于混沌理论的轻量级图像加密方案，用于多模态助听器中的实时音频/视觉数据保护。
AI.motivation: 现有加密算法计算复杂度高，难以满足多模态助听器的低延迟需求，同时云端处理存在隐私风险。
AI.method: 结合Tangent-Delay椭圆反射腔映射系统(TD-ERCS)与非线性混沌算法(NCA)，设计轻量级混沌加密方案。
AI.result: 实验显示该方案在执行时间、密钥空间和抗暴力攻击能力方面优于现有方法，通过多项安全指标测试。
AI.conclusion: 新方案在保证安全性的同时显著降低计算开销，适用于资源受限的多模态助听器设备。

authors_text: Yabin Zhu,Chenglong Li,Yao Liu,Xiao Wang,Jin Tang,Bin Luo,Zhixiang Huang
title: Tiny Object Tracking: A Large-scale Dataset and A Baseline
title_zh: 微小物体跟踪：大规模数据集和基线
abstract: Tiny objects, frequently appearing in practical applications, have weak appearance and features, and receive increasing interests in meany vision tasks, such as object detection and segmentation. To promote the research and development of tiny object tracking, we create a large-scale video dataset, which contains 434 sequences with a total of more than 217K frames. Each frame is carefully annotated with a high-quality bounding box. In data creation, we take 12 challenge attributes into account to cover a broad range of viewpoints and scene complexities, and annotate these attributes for facilitating the attribute-based performance analysis. To provide a strong baseline in tiny object tracking, we propose a novel Multilevel Knowledge Distillation Network (MKDNet), which pursues three-level knowledge distillations in a unified framework to effectively enhance the feature representation, discrimination and localization abilities in tracking tiny objects. Extensive experiments are performed on the proposed dataset, and the results prove the superiority and effectiveness of MKDNet compared with state-of-the-art methods. The dataset, the algorithm code, and the evaluation code are available at https://github.com/mmic-lcl/Datasets-and-benchmark-code.
summary: Tiny objects, frequently appearing in practical applications, have weak appearance and features, and receive increasing interests in meany vision tasks, such as object detection and segmentation. To promote the research and development of tiny object tracking, we create a large-scale video dataset, which contains 434 sequences with a total of more than 217K frames. Each frame is carefully annotated with a high-quality bounding box. In data creation, we take 12 challenge attributes into account to cover a broad range of viewpoints and scene complexities, and annotate these attributes for facilitating the attribute-based performance analysis. To provide a strong baseline in tiny object tracking, we propose a novel Multilevel Knowledge Distillation Network (MKDNet), which pursues three-level knowledge distillations in a unified framework to effectively enhance the feature representation, discrimination and localization abilities in tracking tiny objects. Extensive experiments are performed on the proposed dataset, and the results prove the superiority and effectiveness of MKDNet compared with state-of-the-art methods. The dataset, the algorithm code, and the evaluation code are available at https://github.com/mmic-lcl/Datasets-and-benchmark-code.
abstract_zh: 在实际应用中经常出现的微小物体，其外观和特征较弱，并且在物体检测和分割等复杂的视觉任务中越来越受到人们的关注。为了促进微小物体跟踪的研究和发展，我们创建了一个大规模视频数据集，其中包含 434 个序列，总共超过 217K 帧。每个框架都用高质量的边界框仔细注释。在数据创建中，我们考虑了 12 个挑战属性，以涵盖广泛的观点和场景复杂性，并对这些属性进行注释以促进基于属性的性能分析。为了为微小物体跟踪提供强大的基线，我们提出了一种新颖的多级知识蒸馏网络（MKDNet），它在统一的框架中追求三级知识蒸馏，以有效增强跟踪微小物体的特征表示、辨别和定位能力。在所提出的数据集上进行了大量的实验，结果证明了 MKDNet 与最先进的方法相比的优越性和有效性。数据集、算法代码和评估代码可在 https://github.com/mmic-lcl/Datasets-and-benchmark-code 上获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个大规模数据集和基线方法，用于解决微小目标跟踪中的挑战。
AI.motivation: 微小目标因外观特征弱且易受环境干扰，在视觉任务中跟踪难度大，现有研究缺乏高质量数据集和有效算法。
AI.method: 构建包含434个序列、21.7万帧的高精度标注数据集，并设计多级知识蒸馏网络（MKDNet）增强特征表示与定位能力。
AI.result: 在自建数据集上实验表明，MKDNet相较现有方法在跟踪精度和鲁棒性方面具有显著优势。
AI.conclusion: 该数据集和算法为微小目标跟踪研究提供了基准，推动了相关技术的发展。

search_text: Tiny Object Tracking: A Large-scale Dataset and A Baseline 微小物体跟踪：大规模数据集和基线 Tiny objects, frequently appearing in practical applications, have weak appearance and features, and receive increasing interests in meany vision tasks, such as object detection and segmentation. To promote the research and development of tiny object tracking, we create a large-scale video dataset, which contains 434 sequences with a total of more than 217K frames. Each frame is carefully annotated with a high-quality bounding box. In data creation, we take 12 challenge attributes into account to cover a broad range of viewpoints and scene complexities, and annotate these attributes for facilitating the attribute-based performance analysis. To provide a strong baseline in tiny object tracking, we propose a novel Multilevel Knowledge Distillation Network (MKDNet), which pursues three-level knowledge distillations in a unified framework to effectively enhance the feature representation, discrimination and localization abilities in tracking tiny objects. Extensive experiments are performed on the proposed dataset, and the results prove the superiority and effectiveness of MKDNet compared with state-of-the-art methods. The dataset, the algorithm code, and the evaluation code are available at https://github.com/mmic-lcl/Datasets-and-benchmark-code. 在实际应用中经常出现的微小物体，其外观和特征较弱，并且在物体检测和分割等复杂的视觉任务中越来越受到人们的关注。为了促进微小物体跟踪的研究和发展，我们创建了一个大规模视频数据集，其中包含 434 个序列，总共超过 217K 帧。每个框架都用高质量的边界框仔细注释。在数据创建中，我们考虑了 12 个挑战属性，以涵盖广泛的观点和场景复杂性，并对这些属性进行注释以促进基于属性的性能分析。为了为微小物体跟踪提供强大的基线，我们提出了一种新颖的多级知识蒸馏网络（MKDNet），它在统一的框架中追求三级知识蒸馏，以有效增强跟踪微小物体的特征表示、辨别和定位能力。在所提出的数据集上进行了大量的实验，结果证明了 MKDNet 与最先进的方法相比的优越性和有效性。数据集、算法代码和评估代码可在 https://github.com/mmic-lcl/Datasets-and-benchmark-code 上获取。 本文提出一个大规模数据集和基线方法，用于解决微小目标跟踪中的挑战。 Yabin Zhu,Chenglong Li,Yao Liu,Xiao Wang,Jin Tang,Bin Luo,Zhixiang Huang cs.CV

title: Evaluation of post-hoc interpretability methods in time-series classification
title_zh: 时间序列分类中事后可解释性方法的评估
comment: New version to match published version in Nature Machine Intelligence
comments: New version to match published version in Nature Machine Intelligence
abstract: Post-hoc interpretability methods are critical tools to explain neural-network results. Several post-hoc methods have emerged in recent years, but when applied to a given task, they produce different results, raising the question of which method is the most suitable to provide correct post-hoc interpretability. To understand the performance of each method, quantitative evaluation of interpretability methods is essential. However, currently available frameworks have several drawbacks which hinders the adoption of post-hoc interpretability methods, especially in high-risk sectors. In this work, we propose a framework with quantitative metrics to assess the performance of existing post-hoc interpretability methods in particular in time series classification. We show that several drawbacks identified in the literature are addressed, namely dependence on human judgement, retraining, and shift in the data distribution when occluding samples. We additionally design a synthetic dataset with known discriminative features and tunable complexity. The proposed methodology and quantitative metrics can be used to understand the reliability of interpretability methods results obtained in practical applications. In turn, they can be embedded within operational workflows in critical fields that require accurate interpretability results for e.g., regulatory policies.

We show that several drawbacks identified in the literature are addressed, namely dependence on human judgement, retraining, and shift in the data distribution when occluding samples. We additionally design a synthetic dataset with known discriminative features and tunable complexity. The proposed methodology and quantitative metrics can be used to understand the reliability of interpretability methods results obtained in practical applications. abstract_zh: 事后可解释性方法是解释神经网络结果的关键工具。近年来出现了几种事后方法，但当应用于给定任务时，它们会产生不同的结果，这就提出了哪种方法最适合提供正确的事后可解释性的问题。为了了解每种方法的性能，对可解释性方法的定量评估至关重要。然而，当前可用的框架有几个缺点，阻碍了事后可解释性方法的采用，特别是在高风险领域。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个具有定量指标的框架来评估现有事后可解释性方法的性能，特别是在时间序列分类方面。我们表明，文献中发现的几个缺点得到了解决，即依赖于人类判断、再训练以及遮挡样本时数据分布的变化。我们还设计了一个具有已知判别特征和可调复杂性的合成数据集。所提出的方法和定量指标可用于了解实际应用中获得的可解释性方法结果的可靠性。反过来，它们可以嵌入关键领域的操作工作流程中，这些领域需要准确的可解释性结果，例如监管政策。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种评估后验可解释性方法在时间序列分类中性能的框架，通过量化指标解决现有方法的局限性。
AI.motivation: 现有后验可解释性方法在高风险领域应用时存在依赖人工判断、需要重新训练及数据分布偏移等问题，缺乏统一的量化评估标准。
AI.method: 设计包含已知判别特征和可调复杂度的合成数据集，结合量化指标框架评估方法性能，解决依赖人工、重新训练及数据偏移等缺陷。
AI.result: 框架有效解决了文献中指出的问题，能够可靠评估解释性方法在实际应用中的结果可靠性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法可嵌入关键领域操作流程，为监管政策等需要精确解释性的场景提供可靠评估工具。

title: Vehicle and License Plate Recognition with Novel Dataset for Toll Collection
title_zh: 使用新颖的收费数据集进行车辆和车牌识别
abstract: We propose an automatic framework for toll collection, consisting of three steps: vehicle type recognition, license plate localization, and reading. However, each of the three steps becomes non-trivial due to image variations caused by several factors. The traditional vehicle decorations on the front cause variations among vehicles of the same type. These decorations make license plate localization and recognition difficult due to severe background clutter and partial occlusions. Likewise, on most vehicles, specifically trucks, the position of the license plate is not consistent. Lastly, for license plate reading, the variations are induced by non-uniform font styles, sizes, and partially occluded letters and numbers. Our proposed framework takes advantage of both data availability and performance evaluation of the backbone deep learning architectures. We gather a novel dataset, \emph{Diverse Vehicle and License Plates Dataset (DVLPD)}, consisting of 10k images belonging to six vehicle types. Each image is then manually annotated for vehicle type, license plate, and its characters and digits. For each of the three tasks, we evaluate You Only Look Once (YOLO)v2, YOLOv3, YOLOv4, and FasterRCNN. For real-time implementation on a Raspberry Pi, we evaluate the lighter versions of YOLO named Tiny YOLOv3 and Tiny YOLOv4. The best Mean Average Precision (mAP@0.5) of 98.8% for vehicle type recognition, 98.5% for license plate detection, and 98.3% for license plate reading is achieved by YOLOv4, while its lighter version, i.e., Tiny YOLOv4 obtained a mAP of 97.1%, 97.4%, and 93.7% on vehicle type recognition, license plate detection, and license plate reading, respectively. The dataset and the training codes are available at https://github.com/usama-x930/VT-LPR

However, each of the three steps becomes non-trivial due to image variations caused by several factors. The traditional vehicle decorations on the front cause variations among vehicles of the same type. These decorations make license plate localization and recognition difficult due to severe background clutter and partial occlusions. Likewise, on most vehicles, specifically trucks, the position of the license plate is not consistent. Lastly, for license plate reading, the variations are induced by non-uniform font styles, sizes, and partially occluded letters and numbers. Our proposed framework takes advantage of both data availability and performance evaluation of the backbone deep learning architectures. We gather a novel dataset, \emph{Diverse Vehicle and License Plates Dataset (DVLPD)}, consisting of 10k images belonging to six vehicle types. Each image is then manually annotated for vehicle type, license plate, and its characters and digits. For each of the three tasks, we evaluate You Only Look Once (YOLO)v2, YOLOv3, YOLOv4, and FasterRCNN. For real-time implementation on a Raspberry Pi, we evaluate the lighter versions of YOLO named Tiny YOLOv3 and Tiny YOLOv4. The best Mean Average Precision (mAP@0.5) of 98.8% for vehicle type recognition, 98.5% for license plate detection, and 98.3% for license plate reading is achieved by YOLOv4, while its lighter version, i.e., Tiny YOLOv4 obtained a mAP of 97.1%, 97.4%, and 93.7% on vehicle type recognition, license plate detection, and license plate reading, respectively. The dataset and the training codes are available at https://github.com/usama-x930/VT-LPR
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一个自动收费框架，包括三个步骤：车辆类型识别、车牌定位和读取。然而，由于多种因素导致图像变化，这三个步骤中的每一个都变得非常重要。传统的车辆前部装饰导致同类型车辆之间存在差异。由于严重的背景混乱和部分遮挡，这些装饰使得车牌定位和识别变得困难。同样，在大多数车辆上，特别是卡车，车牌的位置也不一致。最后，对于车牌读取，变化是由不统一的字体样式、大小以及部分遮挡的字母和数字引起的。我们提出的框架利用了骨干深度学习架构的数据可用性和性能评估。我们收集了一个新颖的数据集，\emph{多样化车辆和车牌数据集（DVLPD）}，由属于六种车辆类型的 10k 图像组成。然后，每个图像都会手动注释车辆类型、车牌及其字符和数字。对于这三个任务中的每一个，我们评估 You Only Look Once (YOLO)v2、YOLOv3、YOLOv4 和 FasterRCNN。为了在 Raspberry Pi 上实时实现，我们评估了 YOLO 的轻量级版本，名为 Tiny YOLOv3 和 Tiny YOLOv4。 YOLOv4 的最佳平均精度 (mAP@0.5) 为 98.8%，车牌检测为 98.5%，车牌读取为 98.3%，而其较轻版本 Tiny YOLOv4 在车型识别、车牌检测和车牌读取方面分别获得了 97.1%、97.4% 和 93.7% 的 mAP。数据集和训练代码可在 https://github.com/usama-x930/VT-LPR 获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种用于自动收费的框架，包含车辆类型识别、车牌定位和读取三个步骤，并构建了新型数据集DVLPD进行评估。
AI.motivation: 传统车辆装饰、车牌位置不一致及字体样式差异导致车牌识别困难，需通过新型数据集和深度学习方法提升准确率。
AI.method: 构建包含10k张图像的DVLPD数据集，手动标注车辆类型及车牌信息；采用YOLOv2/v3/v4、FasterRCNN等模型进行多任务评估，并测试轻量化版本在树莓派上的实时性能。
AI.result: YOLOv4在车辆识别（98.8%）、车牌检测（98.5%）、车牌读取（98.3%）中表现最佳；Tiny YOLOv4分别达到97.1%、97.4%、93.7%。
AI.conclusion: YOLOv4在准确率上优于其他模型，轻量化版本适合边缘设备部署，数据集和代码已开源。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种用于自动收费的框架，包含车辆类型识别、车牌定位和读取三个步骤，并构建了新型数据集DVLPD进行评估。
ai_content.motivation: 传统车辆装饰、车牌位置不一致及字体样式差异导致车牌识别困难，需通过新型数据集和深度学习方法提升准确率。
ai_content.method: 构建包含10k张图像的DVLPD数据集，手动标注车辆类型及车牌信息；采用YOLOv2/v3/v4、FasterRCNN等模型进行多任务评估，并测试轻量化版本在树莓派上的实时性能。
ai_content.result: YOLOv4在车辆识别（98.8%）、车牌检测（98.5%）、车牌读取（98.3%）中表现最佳；Tiny YOLOv4分别达到97.1%、97.4%、93.7%。
ai_content.conclusion: YOLOv4在准确率上优于其他模型，轻量化版本适合边缘设备部署，数据集和代码已开源。

search_text: Vehicle and License Plate Recognition with Novel Dataset for Toll Collection 使用新颖的收费数据集进行车辆和车牌识别 We propose an automatic framework for toll collection, consisting of three steps: vehicle type recognition, license plate localization, and reading. However, each of the three steps becomes non-trivial due to image variations caused by several factors. The traditional vehicle decorations on the front cause variations among vehicles of the same type. These decorations make license plate localization and recognition difficult due to severe background clutter and partial occlusions. Likewise, on most vehicles, specifically trucks, the position of the license plate is not consistent. Lastly, for license plate reading, the variations are induced by non-uniform font styles, sizes, and partially occluded letters and numbers. Our proposed framework takes advantage of both data availability and performance evaluation of the backbone deep learning architectures. We gather a novel dataset, \emph{Diverse Vehicle and License Plates Dataset (DVLPD)}, consisting of 10k images belonging to six vehicle types. Each image is then manually annotated for vehicle type, license plate, and its characters and digits. For each of the three tasks, we evaluate You Only Look Once (YOLO)v2, YOLOv3, YOLOv4, and FasterRCNN. For real-time implementation on a Raspberry Pi, we evaluate the lighter versions of YOLO named Tiny YOLOv3 and Tiny YOLOv4. The best Mean Average Precision (mAP@0.5) of 98.8% for vehicle type recognition, 98.5% for license plate detection, and 98.3% for license plate reading is achieved by YOLOv4, while its lighter version, i.e., Tiny YOLOv4 obtained a mAP of 97.1%, 97.4%, and 93.7% on vehicle type recognition, license plate detection, and license plate reading, respectively. The dataset and the training codes are available at https://github.com/usama-x930/VT-LPR 我们提出了一个自动收费框架，包括三个步骤：车辆类型识别、车牌定位和读取。然而，由于多种因素导致图像变化，这三个步骤中的每一个都变得非常重要。传统的车辆前部装饰导致同类型车辆之间存在差异。由于严重的背景混乱和部分遮挡，这些装饰使得车牌定位和识别变得困难。同样，在大多数车辆上，特别是卡车，车牌的位置也不一致。最后，对于车牌读取，变化是由不统一的字体样式、大小以及部分遮挡的字母和数字引起的。我们提出的框架利用了骨干深度学习架构的数据可用性和性能评估。我们收集了一个新颖的数据集，\emph{多样化车辆和车牌数据集（DVLPD）}，由属于六种车辆类型的 10k 图像组成。然后，每个图像都会手动注释车辆类型、车牌及其字符和数字。对于这三个任务中的每一个，我们评估 You Only Look Once (YOLO)v2、YOLOv3、YOLOv4 和 FasterRCNN。为了在 Raspberry Pi 上实时实现，我们评估了 YOLO 的轻量级版本，名为 Tiny YOLOv3 和 Tiny YOLOv4。 YOLOv4 的最佳平均精度 (mAP@0.5) 为 98.8%，车牌检测为 98.5%，车牌读取为 98.3%，而其较轻版本 Tiny YOLOv4 在车型识别、车牌检测和车牌读取方面分别获得了 97.1%、97.4% 和 93.7% 的 mAP。数据集和训练代码可在 https://github.com/usama-x930/VT-LPR 获取 本文提出一种用于自动收费的框架，包含车辆类型识别、车牌定位和读取三个步骤，并构建了新型数据集DVLPD进行评估。 Muhammad Usama,Hafeez Anwar,Abbas Anwar,Saeed Anwar eess.IV,cs.AI,cs.CV

authors_text: Haimin Luo,Teng Xu,Yuheng Jiang,Chenglin Zhou,Qiwei Qiu,Yingliang Zhang,Wei Yang,Lan Xu,Jingyi Yu
title: Artemis: Articulated Neural Pets with Appearance and Motion synthesis
title_zh: Artemis：具有外观和运动合成的关节式神经宠物
abstract: We, humans, are entering into a virtual era and indeed want to bring animals to the virtual world as well for companion. Yet, computer-generated (CGI) furry animals are limited by tedious off-line rendering, let alone interactive motion control. In this paper, we present ARTEMIS, a novel neural modeling and rendering pipeline for generating ARTiculated neural pets with appEarance and Motion synthesIS. Our ARTEMIS enables interactive motion control, real-time animation, and photo-realistic rendering of furry animals. The core of our ARTEMIS is a neural-generated (NGI) animal engine, which adopts an efficient octree-based representation for animal animation and fur rendering. The animation then becomes equivalent to voxel-level deformation based on explicit skeletal warping. We further use a fast octree indexing and efficient volumetric rendering scheme to generate appearance and density features maps. Finally, we propose a novel shading network to generate high-fidelity details of appearance and opacity under novel poses from appearance and density feature maps. For the motion control module in ARTEMIS, we combine state-of-the-art animal motion capture approach with recent neural character control scheme. We introduce an effective optimization scheme to reconstruct the skeletal motion of real animals captured by a multi-view RGB and Vicon camera array. We feed all the captured motion into a neural character control scheme to generate abstract control signals with motion styles. We further integrate ARTEMIS into existing engines that support VR headsets, providing an unprecedented immersive experience where a user can intimately interact with a variety of virtual animals with vivid movements and photo-realistic appearance. We make available our ARTEMIS model and dynamic furry animal dataset at https://haiminluo.github.io/publication/artemis/.

Yet, computer-generated (CGI) furry animals are limited by tedious off-line rendering, let alone interactive motion control. In this paper, we present ARTEMIS, a novel neural modeling and rendering pipeline for generating ARTiculated neural pets with appEarance and Motion synthesIS. Our ARTEMIS enables interactive motion control, real-time animation, and photo-realistic rendering of furry animals. The core of our ARTEMIS is a neural-generated (NGI) animal engine, which adopts an efficient octree-based representation for animal animation and fur rendering. The animation then becomes equivalent to voxel-level deformation based on explicit skeletal warping. We further use a fast octree indexing and efficient volumetric rendering scheme to generate appearance and density features maps. Finally, we propose a novel shading network to generate high-fidelity details of appearance and opacity under novel poses from appearance and density feature maps. For the motion control module in ARTEMIS, we combine state-of-the-art animal motion capture approach with recent neural character control scheme. We introduce an effective optimization scheme to reconstruct the skeletal motion of real animals captured by a multi-view RGB and Vicon camera array. We feed all the captured motion into a neural character control scheme to generate abstract control signals with motion styles. We further integrate ARTEMIS into existing engines that support VR headsets, providing an unprecedented immersive experience where a user can intimately interact with a variety of virtual animals with vivid movements and photo-realistic appearance. We make available our ARTEMIS model and dynamic furry animal dataset at https://haiminluo.github.io/publication/artemis/.
abstract_zh: 我们人类正在进入虚拟时代，也确实想把动物也带入虚拟世界作为伴侣。然而，计算机生成 (CGI) 毛茸茸的动物受到繁琐的离线渲染的限制，更不用说交互式运动控制了。在本文中，我们提出了 ARTEMIS，一种新颖的神经建模和渲染管道，用于通过 AppEarance 和 Motion SynthesIS 生成 ARTiculated 神经宠物。我们的 ARTEMIS 可实现毛茸茸动物的交互式运动控制、实时动画和照片级逼真渲染。我们的 ARTEMIS 的核心是神经生成（NGI）动物引擎，它采用基于八叉树的高效表示来进行动物动画和毛皮渲染。然后，动画就相当于基于显式骨骼扭曲的体素级变形。我们进一步使用快速八叉树索引和高效的体积渲染方案来生成外观和密度特征图。最后，我们提出了一种新颖的着色网络，可以根据外观和密度特征图生成新颖姿势下的外观和不透明度的高保真细节。对于 ARTEMIS 中的运动控制模块，我们将最先进的动物运动捕捉方法与最新的神经角色控制方案相结合。我们引入了一种有效的优化方案来重建由多视图 RGB 和 Vicon 相机阵列捕获的真实动物的骨骼运动。我们将所有捕获的运动输入神经角色控制方案中，以生成具有运动风格的抽象控制信号。我们进一步将ARTEMIS集成到支持VR耳机的现有引擎中，提供前所未有的沉浸式体验，用户可以通过生动的动作和逼真的外观与各种虚拟动物亲密互动。我们在 https://haiminluo.github.io/publication/artemis/ 上提供了 ARTEMIS 模型和动态毛茸茸动物数据集。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ARTEMIS系统，通过神经网络生成具有逼真外观和动态控制的虚拟宠物，实现实时渲染与交互式动画。
AI.motivation: 传统CGI动物受限于离线渲染和缺乏交互控制，难以满足虚拟时代对虚拟宠物的沉浸式需求。
AI.method: 基于八叉树结构构建神经动物引擎，结合骨骼变形与体积渲染生成外观特征，并融合动作捕捉与神经控制方案实现动态交互。
AI.result: 实现实时逼真渲染、多视角VR交互及动态毛发效果，开源模型与数据集提升虚拟宠物研究基础。
AI.conclusion: ARTEMIS通过神经渲染与控制技术突破，为虚拟动物交互提供高保真解决方案，推动VR沉浸体验发展。

authors_text: Dat Ngo,Lam Pham,Truong Hoang,Sefki Kolozali,Delaram Jarchi
title: Audio-Based Deep Learning Frameworks for Detecting COVID-19
title_zh: 用于检测 COVID-19 的基于音频的深度学习框架
abstract: This paper evaluates a wide range of audio-based deep learning frameworks applied to the breathing, cough, and speech sounds for detecting COVID-19. In general, the audio recording inputs are transformed into low-level spectrogram features, then they are fed into pre-trained deep learning models to extract high-level embedding features. Next, the dimension of these high-level embedding features are reduced before finetuning using Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) as a back-end classification. Our experiments on the Second DiCOVA Challenge achieved the highest Area Under the Curve (AUC), F1 score, sensitivity score, and specificity score of 89.03%, 64.41%, 63.33%, and 95.13%, respectively. Based on these scores, our method outperforms the state-of-the-art systems, and improves the challenge baseline by 4.33%, 6.00% and 8.33% in terms of AUC, F1 score and sensitivity score, respectively.
summary: This paper evaluates a wide range of audio-based deep learning frameworks applied to the breathing, cough, and speech sounds for detecting COVID-19. In general, the audio recording inputs are transformed into low-level spectrogram features, then they are fed into pre-trained deep learning models to extract high-level embedding features. Next, the dimension of these high-level embedding features are reduced before finetuning using Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) as a back-end classification. Our experiments on the Second DiCOVA Challenge achieved the highest Area Under the Curve (AUC), F1 score, sensitivity score, and specificity score of 89.03%, 64.41%, 63.33%, and 95.13%, respectively. Based on these scores, our method outperforms the state-of-the-art systems, and improves the challenge baseline by 4.33%, 6.00% and 8.33% in terms of AUC, F1 score and sensitivity score, respectively.
abstract_zh: 本文评估了各种基于音频的深度学习框架，这些框架应用于检测 COVID-19 的呼吸、咳嗽和语音。一般来说，录音输入被转换为低级频谱图特征，然后将它们输入到预先训练的深度学习模型中以提取高级嵌入特征。接下来，在使用轻梯度增强机（LightGBM）作为后端分类进行微调之前，会降低这些高级嵌入特征的维度。我们在第二次 DiCOVA 挑战中的实验取得了最高的曲线下面积 (AUC)、F1 评分、敏感性评分和特异性评分，分别为 89.03%、64.41%、63.33% 和 95.13%。基于这些分数，我们的方法优于最先进的系统，并且在 AUC、F1 分数和敏感性分数方面分别将挑战基线提高了 4.33%、6.00% 和 8.33%。

AI.tldr: 本文评估了多种基于音频的深度学习框架，用于通过呼吸、咳嗽和语音声音检测 COVID-19。
AI.motivation: 开发一种非侵入性、基于音频的 COVID-19 检测方法，以提高筛查效率和可及性。
AI.method: 将音频输入转换为频谱图特征，利用预训练深度学习模型提取高层嵌入特征，降维后结合 LightGBM 进行分类优化。
AI.result: 在 Second DiCOVA 挑战中，AUC 达 89.03%，F1 分数 64.41%，灵敏度 63.33%，特异性 95.13%，较基线提升 4.33%-8.33%。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在音频驱动的 COVID-19 检测中优于现有技术，验证了深度学习与传统模型结合的有效性。

ai_content.tldr: 本文评估了多种基于音频的深度学习框架，用于通过呼吸、咳嗽和语音声音检测 COVID-19。
ai_content.motivation: 开发一种非侵入性、基于音频的 COVID-19 检测方法，以提高筛查效率和可及性。
ai_content.method: 将音频输入转换为频谱图特征，利用预训练深度学习模型提取高层嵌入特征，降维后结合 LightGBM 进行分类优化。
ai_content.result: 在 Second DiCOVA 挑战中，AUC 达 89.03%，F1 分数 64.41%，灵敏度 63.33%，特异性 95.13%，较基线提升 4.33%-8.33%。
ai_content.conclusion: 该方法在音频驱动的 COVID-19 检测中优于现有技术，验证了深度学习与传统模型结合的有效性。

search_text: Audio-Based Deep Learning Frameworks for Detecting COVID-19 用于检测 COVID-19 的基于音频的深度学习框架 This paper evaluates a wide range of audio-based deep learning frameworks applied to the breathing, cough, and speech sounds for detecting COVID-19. In general, the audio recording inputs are transformed into low-level spectrogram features, then they are fed into pre-trained deep learning models to extract high-level embedding features. Next, the dimension of these high-level embedding features are reduced before finetuning using Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) as a back-end classification. Our experiments on the Second DiCOVA Challenge achieved the highest Area Under the Curve (AUC), F1 score, sensitivity score, and specificity score of 89.03%, 64.41%, 63.33%, and 95.13%, respectively. Based on these scores, our method outperforms the state-of-the-art systems, and improves the challenge baseline by 4.33%, 6.00% and 8.33% in terms of AUC, F1 score and sensitivity score, respectively. 本文评估了各种基于音频的深度学习框架，这些框架应用于检测 COVID-19 的呼吸、咳嗽和语音。一般来说，录音输入被转换为低级频谱图特征，然后将它们输入到预先训练的深度学习模型中以提取高级嵌入特征。接下来，在使用轻梯度增强机（LightGBM）作为后端分类进行微调之前，会降低这些高级嵌入特征的维度。我们在第二次 DiCOVA 挑战中的实验取得了最高的曲线下面积 (AUC)、F1 评分、敏感性评分和特异性评分，分别为 89.03%、64.41%、63.33% 和 95.13%。基于这些分数，我们的方法优于最先进的系统，并且在 AUC、F1 分数和敏感性分数方面分别将挑战基线提高了 4.33%、6.00% 和 8.33%。 本文评估了多种基于音频的深度学习框架，用于通过呼吸、咳嗽和语音声音检测 COVID-19。 Dat Ngo,Lam Pham,Truong Hoang,Sefki Kolozali,Delaram Jarchi cs.SD,eess.AS

title: A Wasserstein GAN for Joint Learning of Inpainting and Spatial Optimisation
title_zh: 用于修复和空间优化联合学习的 Wasserstein GAN
abstract: Image inpainting is a restoration method that reconstructs missing image parts. However, a carefully selected mask of known pixels that yield a high quality inpainting can also act as a sparse image representation. This challenging spatial optimisation problem is essential for practical applications such as compression. So far, it has been almost exclusively adressed by model-based approaches. First attempts with neural networks seem promising, but are tailored towards specific inpainting operators or require postprocessing. To address this issue, we propose the first generative adversarial network (GAN) for spatial inpainting data optimisation. In contrast to previous approaches, it allows joint training of an inpainting generator and a corresponding mask optimisation network. With a Wasserstein distance, we ensure that our inpainting results accurately reflect the statistics of natural images. This yields significant improvements in visual quality and speed over conventional stochastic models. It also outperforms current spatial optimisation networks.
summary: Image inpainting is a restoration method that reconstructs missing image parts. However, a carefully selected mask of known pixels that yield a high quality inpainting can also act as a sparse image representation. This challenging spatial optimisation problem is essential for practical applications such as compression. So far, it has been almost exclusively adressed by model-based approaches. First attempts with neural networks seem promising, but are tailored towards specific inpainting operators or require postprocessing. To address this issue, we propose the first generative adversarial network (GAN) for spatial inpainting data optimisation. In contrast to previous approaches, it allows joint training of an inpainting generator and a corresponding mask optimisation network. With a Wasserstein distance, we ensure that our inpainting results accurately reflect the statistics of natural images. This yields significant improvements in visual quality and speed over conventional stochastic models. It also outperforms current spatial optimisation networks.
abstract_zh: 图像修复是一种重建丢失图像部分的恢复方法。然而，精心选择的已知像素掩模可以产生高质量的修复，也可以充当稀疏图像表示。这一具有挑战性的空间优化问题对于压缩等实际应用至关重要。到目前为止，它几乎完全通过基于模型的方法来解决。神经网络的首次尝试似乎很有希望，但针对特定的修复操作员量身定制或需要后处理。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了第一个用于空间修复数据优化的生成对抗网络（GAN）。与以前的方法相比，它允许联合训练修复生成器和相应的掩模优化网络。通过 Wasserstein 距离，我们确保修复结果准确反映自然图像的统计数据。与传统的随机模型相比，这在视觉质量和速度方面有了显着的提高。它还优于当前的空间优化网络。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于Wasserstein GAN的联合学习框架，用于图像修复与空间优化的协同训练。
AI.motivation: 传统图像修复依赖模型方法，神经网络方法存在适配性差或需后处理的问题，亟需高效联合优化修复与掩码生成的方案。
AI.method: 设计首个联合训练的GAN架构，包含图像修复生成器与掩码优化网络，通过Wasserstein距离约束生成结果符合自然图像统计特性。
AI.result: 在视觉质量与计算速度上显著优于传统随机模型及现有空间优化网络，实现修复效果与掩码优化的同步提升。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过联合学习机制与Wasserstein距离约束，有效解决了图像修复与空间优化的协同问题，为压缩等应用提供新思路。

title: Online Decision Transformer
title_zh: 在线决策转换器
abstract: Recent work has shown that offline reinforcement learning (RL) can be formulated as a sequence modeling problem (Chen et al., 2021; Janner et al., 2021) and solved via approaches similar to large-scale language modeling. However, any practical instantiation of RL also involves an online component, where policies pretrained on passive offline datasets are finetuned via taskspecific interactions with the environment. We propose Online Decision Transformers (ODT), an RL algorithm based on sequence modeling that blends offline pretraining with online finetuning in a unified framework. Our framework uses sequence-level entropy regularizers in conjunction with autoregressive modeling objectives for sample-efficient exploration and finetuning. Empirically, we show that ODT is competitive with the state-of-the-art in absolute performance on the D4RL benchmark but shows much more significant gains during the finetuning procedure.
summary: Recent work has shown that offline reinforcement learning (RL) can be formulated as a sequence modeling problem (Chen et al., 2021; Janner et al., 2021) and solved via approaches similar to large-scale language modeling. However, any practical instantiation of RL also involves an online component, where policies pretrained on passive offline datasets are finetuned via taskspecific interactions with the environment. We propose Online Decision Transformers (ODT), an RL algorithm based on sequence modeling that blends offline pretraining with online finetuning in a unified framework. Our framework uses sequence-level entropy regularizers in conjunction with autoregressive modeling objectives for sample-efficient exploration and finetuning. Empirically, we show that ODT is competitive with the state-of-the-art in absolute performance on the D4RL benchmark but shows much more significant gains during the finetuning procedure.
abstract_zh: 最近的工作表明，离线强化学习（RL）可以表述为序列建模问题（Chen 等人，2021；Janner 等人，2021），并通过类似于大规模语言建模的方法来解决。然而，强化学习的任何实际实例也涉及在线组件，其中在被动离线数据集上预训练的策略通过与环境的特定任务交互进行微调。我们提出了在线决策变换器（ODT），这是一种基于序列建模的强化学习算法，它将离线预训练与在线微调融合在一个统一的框架中。我们的框架使用序列级熵正则化器与自回归建模目标相结合，以实现样本高效的探索和微调。根据经验，我们表明 ODT 在 D4RL 基准上的绝对性能与最先进的技术具有竞争力，但在微调过程中显示出更显着的增益。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合离线预训练与在线微调的强化学习算法Online Decision Transformer（ODT），通过序列建模框架提升样本效率和微调效果。
AI.motivation: 离线强化学习虽可转化为序列建模问题，但实际应用需在线微调。现有方法在样本效率和微调阶段表现不足，需统一框架整合两者优势。
AI.method: 设计Online Decision Transformer框架，联合使用序列级熵正则化与自回归建模目标，实现离线预训练与在线微调的统一优化。
AI.result: 在D4RL基准测试中，ODT的绝对性能与SOTA方法相当，但在微调阶段展现出显著性能提升。
AI.conclusion: ODT通过整合离线预训练与在线微调，在样本效率和微调效果上优于现有方法，为强化学习提供了新的序列建模解决方案。
